stories.headline: At least 21 killed in Russia's strikes on Ukraine on Chernobyl anniversary
stories.summary: Russian strikes killed at least 21 across Ukraine over the weekend and into Monday as Kyiv struck a Russian oil refinery, while Zelenskyy and IAEA warned of renewed nuclear risks at Chernobyl.

stories.headline: War and neglect fuel deadly measles epidemic in Sudan’s Darfur
stories.summary: Measles kills 70 in East Darfur's Labado in a few weeks as healthcare collapse leaves families without medicines.

stories.headline: Oil prices rise as US-Iran peace talks stall
stories.summary: President Trump said the US had cancelled plans to send a team to Pakistan for negotiations.

stories.headline: RBC BlueBay’s Dowding Sees Recession Risk for Europe on Iran War
stories.summary: Europe faces recession if the Strait of Hormuz crisis isn’t resolved within a month, according to Mark Dowding, chief investment officer for fixed income of RBC Bluebay Asset Management.

stories.headline: US strike kills three on alleged narco boat as campaign death toll hits 185
stories.summary: Military video shows boat moving swiftly in water before explosion leaves it in flamesThe US military said on Sunday three men were killed when it struck a boat it claimed was “engaged in narco-trafficking operations” in the Eastern Pacific Ocean.This latest strike – which follows dozens of similar attacks on alleged drug boats in recent months – brings the US campaign’s death toll to at least 185, according to a tally compiled by Agence France-P

stories.headline: Oil prices rise amid stalled US-Iran peace talks
stories.summary: Brent crude rises more than 2 percent after Washington and Tehran fail to hold second round of talks in Pakistan.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Araghchi to meet Putin; Strait of Hormuz remains shut
stories.summary: Iran's foreign minister heads to Russia as Trump says Iranian leaders can call on the phone if they want to talk.

stories.headline: Stock markets flat as oil prices hold above $100 on stalled US–Iran talks
stories.summary: European markets and US futures are trading mostly flat on Monday morning, while Brent crude remains above $100 and Asian markets bounce, as talks between Iran and the US stall.

stories.headline: Kim Jong Un opens memorial for N Korean soldiers killed in Ukraine war
stories.summary: Kim Jong Un and Russian Defence Minister Andrey Belousov unveiled the memorial in Pyongyang.

stories.headline: China blocks Meta’s $2bn purchase of AI group Manus
stories.summary: Regulators had reviewed whether deal violated Beijing’s investment rules

stories.headline: China Blocks Meta’s $2 Billion Acquisition of AI Firm Manus
stories.summary: China has decided to block Meta Platforms Inc.’s $2 billion acquisition of agentic AI startup Manus, a surprise move to unwind a controversial deal that’s drawn fire for the leakage of technology to the US.

stories.headline: US Sanctions on China Oil Giant Turn Up Heat for Teapot Refiners
stories.summary: A US move to sanction one of China’s largest private refiners over ties to Iran will hurt a vast and already embattled petrochemicals sector — but the collateral damage will extend far beyond oil.

stories.headline: Live updates: White House correspondents’ dinner shooting suspect to appear in court, had denounced Trump
stories.summary: The shooting prompted the evacuation of President Donald Trump and other high-profile guests from the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner Saturday. The suspect is in custody.

stories.headline: Mali defence minister killed amid flurry of insurgent attacks
stories.summary: Car bomb kills Sadio Camara at home during coordinated assaults by rebel groups including West African al-Qaida affiliateMali’s defence minister was killed in an attack on his residence, the government said on Sunday, a high-profile fatality during coordinated assaults staged the previous day by insurgents including the West African affiliate of al-Qaida.A car laden with explosives driven by a suicide attacker drove into Sadio Camara’s residence

stories.headline: Russia attacks Odesa, claims Ukraine hit Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant
stories.summary: Ukrainian attack on captured Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant kills one worker, according to managers installed by Russia.

stories.headline: North Korea opens museum commemorating troops killed fighting for Russia
stories.summary: Russia and North Korea deepen ties with plans for military cooperation and a symbolic memorial museum in Pyongyang.

stories.headline: King Charles US visit: What to know about the itinerary, Congress address
stories.summary: King Charles III and Queen Camilla’s visit comes amid US–UK tensions and the 250th anniversary of US independence.

stories.headline: Beijing blocks Meta's $2bn acquisition of Manus after security review
Israel and Hezbollah finding ways to go back to "pre-ceasefire modus operandi" - Lebanese Minister
stories.summary: The truce in Israel and Hezbollah's war in Lebanon is "not holding", with both sides finding ways to return to the "modus operandi of the pre-ceasefire agreement", Lebanese Minister Kamal Shehadi tells Europe Today.

stories.headline: What is Europe doing to avoid a new energy crisis? Ask the Euronews AI chatbot
stories.summary: EU fossil fuel imports have increased by €24 billion since the US-Iran war, with oil prices up 60 per cent and gas 70 per cent. How will the EU’s new AccelerateEU plan avoid a full-scale energy shortage? Ask the Euronews AI chatbot.

stories.headline: Trump says King will be 'very safe' during US visit after security talks
stories.summary: The state visit will go ahead despite concerns raised after a gunman targeted an event attended by the president.

stories.headline: Romanian Ex-Ruling Party Joins Far-Right Bid to Oust Premier
stories.summary: Romania’s former top ruling party will join the far-right opposition in a attempt to topple the prime minister, Ilie Bolojan, in the latest escalation of the political crisis that threatens to derail efforts to cut the European Union’s widest budget gap.

stories.headline: Emirates NBD Plans First Middle East AT1 Bond Sale Since War
stories.summary: Emirates NBD Bank PJSC is looking to sell an Additional Tier 1 bond, in what would be the first offering of the riskiest type of bank debt by a Middle Eastern lender since the start of the war.

stories.headline: Power Trader Whose Error Caused Crash Faces $11 Million Fine
stories.summary: A Swedish power trader behind a major collapse in Finland’s electricity market in 2023 is facing a €9.25 million ($11 million) fine.

stories.headline: Daiwa to Buy Orix Bank for $2.3 Billion in Lending Expansion
stories.summary: Daiwa Securities Group Inc. will buy Orix Corp.’s banking unit for ¥370 billion ($2.3 billion), its largest acquisition in almost two decades, as Japan’s second-biggest brokerage deepens its foray into commercial lending.

stories.headline: Philippines Warns Agencies Not to Send Seamen to Persian Gulf
stories.summary: The Philippines is telling crewing agencies to stop sending its nationals to the Persian Gulf, according to people familiar with the matter, making it more difficult for shipowners to rotate the thousands of seamen stuck on vessels behind the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Traders Buy Pound Protection on Policy, Election and War Risks
stories.summary: Investors are adopting a more cautious approach to the pound, with options traders zeroing in on three distinct risks.

stories.headline: Central Bankers Have Bond Market on Edge: Markets in 3 Minutes
stories.summary: Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson, Tom Mackenzie and Paul Dobson break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade." For up to the minute market intelligence and insight, click MLIV . (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Security at Correspondents’ Dinner Worked as Intended, Experts Say
stories.summary: Officials noted that the suspect never made it into the hotel ballroom, where President Trump and hundreds of journalists were gathered for the White House correspondents’ dinner.

stories.headline: Japan's Daiwa Securities Group to acquire Orix Bank for $2.3bn
Japan eyes expanded warship exports with Mogami-class frigate as template
Pirates seize another vessel off Somali coast as threat level increased
stories.summary: The UK's maritime monitor reports at least four suspected piracy incidents in the past week off the coast of Somalia.

stories.headline: Inside China’s plans to fight in space
stories.summary: From seizing satellites to striking Earth from orbit — Beijing is developing dual-use capabilities in an intensifying arms race with the US

stories.headline: Why the UAE asked Pakistan for its $3.5bn back
stories.summary: Tensions between Abu Dhabi and Islamabad have burst into the open

stories.headline: Healthcare is driving America’s economy
stories.summary: This is a huge industry — and that’s not necessarily a good thing

stories.headline: Reeves set for new growth push after May elections
stories.summary: Allies of UK chancellor seek to brush off talk of upheaval amid Labour Party unrest

stories.headline: CATL Seeks to Raise $5 Billion in Largest HK Share Sale of 2026
stories.summary: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. is seeking to raise about $5 billion from a Hong Kong share placement, according to terms of the deal seen by Bloomberg News, in what is the city’s largest offering yet this year.

stories.headline: Goldman Says Hedge Funds Use Rally in US Stocks to Offload Risk
stories.summary: Hedge funds are using the US equity rally to reduce risk, according to traders on Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s prime brokerage desk.

stories.headline: ECB Could Hike in June If Hormuz Reopens, Goldman Says
stories.summary: Simon Dangoor, deputy CIO of fixed income at Goldman Sachs Asset Management, looks ahead to this week's interest-rate decisions, saying that a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz will present policymakers with a markedly different set of risks by the time they next meet in June. Dangoor speaks on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: China Hongqiao Seeks $1.5 Billion From Convertible Bonds
stories.summary: China’s largest private aluminum producer is seeking to raise 10.2 billion yuan ($1.5 billion) from the sale of bonds that can be converted into stock, according to terms of the deal seen by Bloomberg News, joining the wave of large deals as risk appetite returns following a de-escalation on tensions in the Middle East.

stories.headline: SSE and TotalEnergies UK Wind Farm Delays Contract That Would Cap Profits
stories.summary: One of the UK’s largest offshore wind farms delayed starting a government contract that would cap the price of its electricity, instead choosing to sell power in the market at more profitable rates.

stories.headline: Sun Pharma Buys New Jersey-Based Organon in $12 Billion Deal
stories.summary: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd. has agreed to acquire New York-listed women’s health-care company Organon & Co., expanding its global reach with one of the biggest outbound deals by an Indian company.

stories.headline: Bernie Sanders Pressures Democrats to Reject Super PAC Support in Primaries
stories.summary: The independent senator from Vermont says top Democrats should tell candidates they will otherwise lose access to party resources.

stories.headline: Labor senator deletes Anzac Day video after mistakenly including raunchy rap song in Instagram post
stories.summary: Images in Helen Polley’s post included a marching band, people laying wreaths and ex-serving members giving speeches set to a track by US rapper ChingyGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA federal Labor senator has deleted a social media video which mistakenly included audio of an explicit rap song over a carousel of photos of Anzac Day commemorations.Senator Helen Polley, a former shadow minister and current chair of parli

stories.headline: Taiwan court hands down jail terms in TSMC trade secrets case
stories.summary: Ex-Tokyo Electron employee sentenced to 10 years, four other defendants receive between 10 months and six years.

stories.headline: Tokyo condo boom cools off as interest rates rise
BYD earnings, Japanese airline financials, Genting's New York casino tables
Oil Rises as Stalled US-Iran Peace Talks Keep Hormuz Strait Shut
stories.summary: Oil rose after efforts to resume peace talks over the Iran war stalled, leaving the Strait of Hormuz almost impassable and prolonging the supply disruption that has roiled global markets.

stories.headline: Trump Praises WHCA President Weijia Jiang After Shooting at Dinner
stories.summary: Weijia Jiang, a CBS correspondent, had been sitting next to the president at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner when shots rang out. Soon afterward, she was reporting on the attack.

stories.headline: Mali defence minister killed as country hit by wave of rebel attacks
stories.summary: A wave of coordinated attacks by jihadist militants and separatists has spread through the country.

stories.headline: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.headline: Death toll in Colombia highway bus bomb attack rises to 20
stories.summary: Political violence has escalated in Colombia ahead of next month's presidential election.

stories.headline: Hotel Where Reagan Was Shot in 1981 Becomes a Crime Scene Again
stories.summary: The Washington Hilton was the site of an assassination attempt against the 40th president.

stories.headline: Bomb attack on Colombia highway kills 19 ahead of election
stories.summary: Highway bombing in Colombia’s Cauca kills 19, injures dozens amid a surge in violence ahead of next month’s election.

stories.headline: US Stocks Hold Highs as Busy Earnings Week Starts: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: US stocks held near record highs as optimism persisted that the US and Iran could near a deal to restore Middle East oil flows, with traders also eyeing major megacap tech earnings and central bank decisions this week.

stories.headline: Two killed in strike on alleged drug boat in Pacific Ocean, US military says
stories.summary: The incident is the latest in a series of US strikes on alleged drug trafficking boats.

stories.headline: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
stories.summary: Local elections have been held in the occupied West Bank and in one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not taking part.

stories.headline: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
stories.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.

stories.headline: US military kills two more people in strike on alleged drug boat in Pacific
stories.summary: Small boat destroyed in video posted on social media as US campaign has killed at least 178 people since SeptemberThe US military announced on Friday that it killed two people in an attack on a boat in the eastern Pacific, part of a series of deadly strikes on vessels in recent months which it claims are targeting “narco-trafficking” operations.The US Southern Command declared in a social media post on X that Gen Francis L Donovan directed Joint

stories.headline: Mexico says US agents killed in crash weren't permitted to operate there
stories.summary: The two Americans who reportedly worked for the CIA died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab.

stories.headline: Orbán steps down from Hungarian parliament after landslide defeat
stories.summary: The outgoing prime minister will not take up his seat after leading his party back into opposition.

stories.headline: Katya Adler: Europe's Nato allies push back at reported US threat to Spain
stories.summary: Souring relations between Europe and the US have reared their Medusa-like head again, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

stories.headline: What counts as the woods? Judge axes Nova Scotia’s ban that defied ‘commonsense definitions’
stories.summary: The court sided with a Canadian hiker who deliberately challenged the order imposed to curb spread of wildfiresAs wildfires raged across Nova Scotia last summer, the Canadian province made a simple plea to residents: stay away from the woods.As the situation deteriorated, authorities turned the request into a prohibition: anyone caught hiking under the shade of the forest canopy faced a C$25,000 fine – a figure more than half the average worker’s

stories.headline: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.summary: A 10-day truce between the two countries has been extended by three weeks after talks in Washington.

stories.headline: Thousands at risk after multi-million dollar Everest flood warning system left to rust
stories.summary: The flood warning system at Imja glacial lake has not been maintained since 2016, fearful locals tell BBC.

stories.headline: Woman trapped in poo for three hours after outback toilet collapses
stories.summary: The woman got stuck waist-deep in a pit latrine in the Australian outback after it gave way.

stories.headline: Why is this game only legal across Australia one day a year?
stories.summary: Two-up is a coin toss betting game that was played by Australian soldiers during World War One - it’s now only legal across Australia on 25 April.

stories.headline: Headscarf with a beret: Muslim designers showcase floral dresses and boxy streetwear in Paris
stories.summary: Some young attendees say the event demonstrated a more inclusive French culture taking shape.

stories.headline: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer
stories.summary: The Israeli prime minister says he is "in excellent physical condition" after having a malignant tumour removed.

stories.headline: The Kashmir town trying to win back tourists after a deadly attack
stories.summary: A year after the Pahalgam attack, tourism has slowed in Indian-administered Kashmir, hitting lives and livelihoods.

stories.headline: ‘Look, no hands’: China chases the driverless dream at Beijing car show
stories.summary: As domestic sales slow, manufacturers are investing in AI and seeking growth in technology and in overseas marketsAt the world’s biggest car fair, which opened in Beijing on Friday, there were hundreds of manufacturers, more than 1,000 vehicles, hundreds of thousands of enthusiasts – and hardly anyone behind a wheel.China’s car companies have cornered the domestic electric vehicle market, and are increasingly visible on the global stage. Now they

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill eight Palestinians in Gaza, first responders say
stories.summary: One strike killed three children and two adults near a mosque in the northern town of Beit Lahia on Wednesday evening.

stories.headline: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
stories.summary: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.

stories.headline: Iran says Strait of Hormuz cannot be opened due to ceasefire breaches
stories.summary: Iran's chief negotiator says "violations" by the US and Israel make it impossible to open the strait.

stories.headline: Woman killed by bear in Polish forest, son and local government say
stories.summary: In an extremely rare fatal encounter, the 58-year-old was killed as she was walking in a mountainous area, her son told rescuers.

stories.headline: Moldovan oligarch jailed in $1bn 'theft of the century' case
stories.summary: Vlad Plahotniuc, once Moldova's richest man, was found guilty of partaking in a fraud which involved around 12% of the country's GDP.
